novel_features.name: Following but not following back
novel_features.description: Surface accounts you follow that don't follow you back, with bio, last-active, and engagement context so you can decide who to unfollow.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local JOIN of the followers and following tables. SaaS tools (Circleboom, Fedica) require paid OAuth; existing offline tools require manual ZIP uploads. No CLI offers this free + agent-callable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for asymmetric-relationship audits. The local store makes it a single SQL query instead of a paid dashboard or a manual ZIP export.

novel_features.name: Mutuals (two-way follows)
novel_features.description: Show accounts that follow you AND that you follow back. Filter by recency, language, or follower count.
novel_features.rationale: Local INTERSECT on follow tables. Other tools either lock this behind a paywall or require the official OAuth flow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to identify your reciprocal network for outreach, networking, or community building.

novel_features.name: Who unfollowed me (since)
novel_features.description: Diff follower snapshots over time to find accounts that unfollowed you in the last N days, with their bio and last activity.
novel_features.rationale: Requires persistent snapshot history, only possible because we maintain a follow_snapshots table. SaaS tools charge for this; no CLI offers it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to understand audience churn. Snapshot-based, so no API call is needed for the diff itself.

novel_features.name: Ghost followers (inactive)
novel_features.description: List your followers who haven't tweeted in N days. Detect dead accounts inflating your follower count.
novel_features.rationale: Requires last_tweet_at per follower in the local store. Local SQL filter avoids re-querying the API per check.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for audience hygiene. Helps explain low engagement-per-follower ratios.

novel_features.name: Audit inactive accounts you follow
novel_features.description: Find accounts in your following list that have gone silent for N days. Recommend unfollow candidates.
novel_features.rationale: Inverse of ghost-followers. Local store makes this a single SQL filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to clean up your timeline by unfollowing accounts that no longer post.

novel_features.name: Fans (they follow me, I don't follow back)
novel_features.description: Inverse of not-following-back: people who follow you that you haven't followed back. Useful for reciprocal-follow strategies.
novel_features.rationale: Local SET DIFFERENCE on follow tables.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when growing reciprocal relationships. Filterable by recency to focus on new followers.

novel_features.name: Mutual followers between two users
novel_features.description: Find accounts that follow BOTH user A and user B. Useful for network mapping, partnership outreach, OSINT.
novel_features.rationale: Requires syncing followers of both users into the local store, then INTERSECT. Twiangulate's flagship feature, but as an automatable CLI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for warm-intro discovery, audience comparison, or community analysis between any two accounts.

novel_features.name: Suspicious follower audit (bots)
novel_features.description: Heuristic bot detection: missing profile pic, default-pattern names, recent account age, high follow-to-follower ratio, no original tweets.
novel_features.rationale: All inputs computable from the local store. FollowerAudit-class analytics for free.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this to estimate fake-follower percentage and identify likely bots polluting your audience.

novel_features.name: New followers since
novel_features.description: Diff follower snapshots forward to surface newly-acquired followers in the last N days.
novel_features.rationale: Counterpart to unfollowed-me. Requires snapshot history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to acknowledge new followers and detect spikes in audience growth.

novel_features.name: Tweet engagement leaderboard
novel_features.description: Local SQL over synced tweets: rank by likes + 2x retweets + 3x replies. Filter by user, language, media presence, or time window.
novel_features.rationale: Local-only analytics over your synced tweet store. No rate limits on repeated queries; composable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this for content-performance analysis without re-querying the API for each report.

novel_features.name: Whois (aggregated profile)
novel_features.description: One command stitching profile, post velocity, engagement rate, mutuals with you, last active, and top recent tweets into a single agent-friendly view.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates local store + one live API call. Saves agents from making 5-6 separate calls to assemble a profile picture.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as the first call when researching an account; subsequent commands can drill into specifics.

novel_features.name: Saved search (search local store)
novel_features.description: Search your synced tweet store with FTS5: regex, substring, by user, by language, by media presence. No rate limits, fully composable.
novel_features.rationale: Local FTS5 over synced tweets. Re-running the same query against the live API would burn rate limit; against the store it's free and fast.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when iterating on the same query, building dashboards, or running analyses that need many search variations.

novel_features.name: Twitter archive ZIP import
novel_features.description: Bootstrap your local SQLite store from a Twitter data archive ZIP (the free download X provides on request). No rate-limit-bound sync needed for historical tweets, likes, DMs, blocks, mutes.
novel_features.rationale: X provides a complete archive on request. Importing it directly populates years of historical data instantly, sidestepping API pagination and rate limits. Birdclaw-class onboarding.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this as the first step when onboarding — it bootstraps the entire local store from a free archive download instead of weeks of rate-limited sync.

novel_features.name: Git-friendly JSONL export with yearly shards
novel_features.description: Export tweets, DMs, or follows as version-controllable JSONL files, partitioned by year. Pairs with `--format markdown` for human-readable backups.
novel_features.rationale: JSONL is text-diffable — a year-by-year shard layout lets you commit your X archive to Git and see what changed week-over-week.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for durable, text-diffable backups of your X data that survive any tool change.

narrative.headline: Use X from your terminal with your browser session — no paid API key, with a local SQLite database that powers relationship analytics no other tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: x-twitter is a CLI for X (formerly Twitter) that authenticates via your existing browser cookies — no $100/month API tier required. It mirrors every endpoint from the internal GraphQL API into commands, syncs your followers, following, tweets, and bookmarks into a local SQLite store, and unlocks relationship analytics like 'who isn't following me back', 'mutuals', and 'who unfollowed me' that no free tool offers. Every command is agent-native (--json, --select, exit codes) and exposed through MCP for Claude Desktop and other agent clients.
narrative.auth_narrative: x-twitter uses cookie-based auth captured from your logged-in browser. Run `x-twitter auth login --chrome` to import your X session cookies (auth_token, ct0, guest_id) from Chrome. The CLI then signs every request with the matching x-csrf-token header and the standard X web-client bearer token. No paid X API tier needed. Cookies expire — re-run `auth login --chrome` whenever `doctor` reports an expired session.

quickstart.comment: Capture session cookies from your logged-in Chrome browser. No API key needed.
Verify auth, network, rate-limit headroom, and DB integrity.
Mirror your follower list into local SQLite. Required before relationship analytics.
Mirror who you follow into local SQLite. Required before relationship analytics.
The killer feature: who you follow that doesn't follow you back.
Aggregated profile + relationship + engagement summary in one call.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor reports 'session expired' or commands return 401
troubleshoots.fix: Cookies have expired. Re-run `x-twitter auth login --chrome` to capture fresh cookies from your browser.

troubleshoots.symptom: 'csrf cookie does not match header' (error code 353)
troubleshoots.fix: Your ct0 cookie rotated. Re-run `auth login --chrome` to refresh.
Sync resumes from the last cursor. Re-run the sync command after the reset window in the error message.
X rotates GraphQL query IDs occasionally. Run `x-twitter doctor --refresh-ids` to fetch fresh IDs from the spec source.
Run `x-twitter sync followers` and `x-twitter sync following` first — relationship commands query the local store.

narrative.when_to_use: Use x-twitter when you need scriptable, agent-driven access to X without paying for the V2 API tier. It excels at relationship analytics (who isn't following you back, who unfollowed you, mutuals overlap between two users), audience hygiene (ghost followers, suspicious-bot detection), and local-store-backed analytics (engagement leaderboards, saved search). For one-off interactive browsing the X web UI is faster, but for any repeated query, automation, or agent workflow x-twitter is a much better fit because it caches data locally and exposes everything as JSON.

recipes.title: Find who you follow that doesn't follow back
recipes.command: x-twitter sync followers && x-twitter sync following && x-twitter relationships not-following-back --json --select handle,name,follower_count,last_tweet_at | jq '.[] | select(.follower_count > 1000)'
recipes.explanation: Sync both directions, then list the asymmetric edges, narrowing to influencers.

recipes.title: Track who unfollowed you this week
recipes.command: x-twitter sync followers && x-twitter relationships unfollowed-me --since 7d --json
recipes.explanation: Each sync builds a follower snapshot; the diff command surfaces the deltas.

recipes.title: Audit your most engaged tweets last month
recipes.command: x-twitter sync tweets --user me && x-twitter tweets engagement --top 20 --since 30d --user me --json --select id,text,like_count,retweet_count,reply_count
recipes.explanation: Local SQL ranks your tweets by weighted engagement without burning rate limit.

recipes.title: Find common followers between two users (warm intro discovery)
recipes.command: x-twitter sync followers --user @paulg && x-twitter sync followers --user @sama && x-twitter relationships overlap @paulg @sama --json --limit 50
recipes.explanation: Sync both users' followers, then INTERSECT them to find who follows both — useful for partnership outreach.

recipes.title: Whois with deeply-nested response narrowing
recipes.explanation: Whois returns nested data; --select with dotted paths lets agents fetch only the fields they need to keep context small.

trigger_phrases: who isn't following me back on X
